headline: Super vs Grok — personal AI agents for real computer work

site_brief.goal: Create a fair but persuasive competitor comparison page for Super vs Grok. Use supplied sources for current facts, avoid fake claims, and make the practical argument that Super is better suited for repeated computer-use work because its cache can lower repeated execution cost.
site_brief.title: Super vs Grok

summary: Grok is a powerful, opinionated AI assistant with live X (Twitter) data, large context windows, and multi‑agent reasoning modes. Super is built for a different job: durable personal AI agents that actually operate computers and reuse a computer-use cache so repeated workflows get faster and cheaper over time. This page fairly compares where Grok shines — and where Super is the sharper choice for ongoing computer‑use work.
